summary: The analysis confirms multiple critical DoS vulnerabilities in Poetry: (1) No maximum download size limit allows disk exhaustion, (2) Unbounded thread pool exhausts resources on large systems, (3) No solver depth/iteration limits enable CPU/memory exhaustion, (4) Exponential marker computation (2^n) can freeze systems, (5) No download quota system allows cumulative consumption.
expected_security_impact: The demonstration should identify multiple DoS vulnerabilities through static analysis of Poetry's resource management code, confirming lack of limits on downloads, threads, and solver operations.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh analyzed Poetry's code and confirmed 5 critical DoS vulnerabilities: no download size limits, unbounded thread pool, no solver limits, exponential algorithms, and no quota system. All satisfy CVSS 4.0 criteria.
verification_method: Static analysis of Poetry source code to identify missing resource limits, unbounded operations, and exponential complexity. Each finding is verified by inspecting the actual implementation.
evidence_produced_by_run: Downloader class has no max_size parameter or size limit enforcement
ThreadPoolExecutor default max_workers = cpu_count + 4 (132 threads on 128-core server)
VersionSolver.solve() has no max_depth or max_iterations checks
Provider._resolve_overlapping_markers uses itertools.product with repeat=len(dependencies) creating 2^n combinations
Executor has no quota tracking for cumulative download sizes
Report generated with all 5 critical findings
limitations_or_blockers: Actual exploitation not performed to avoid resource exhaustion in test environment
Demonstration uses static analysis instead of active exploitation

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones Poetry repository from GitHub and mounts at /target-repo
